SIP-17: Register new Uniswap and Chainlink providers 

This proposal consists of multiple actions. If the vote is passed, actions will be executed on-chain, resulting in
adding support for new price provider implementations. These providers correspond to Uniswap V3 with support for
multiple pool paths, and Chainlink V3.

## Action 1: Register ChainlinkV3 price provider

The action will register the Chainlink price provider in the Price Providers Repository.

## Action 2: Register UniswapV3 V2 price provider

The action will register the new version of the Uniswap price provider in the Price Providers Repository.

# SIP-15: Move ETH from SiloDAO to the development fund
You are voting to:
1. Move 897 ETH from SiloDAO's treasury to the development fund.
2. Authorize the core team to deposit ETH to silos in response to future snapshot votes.
3. Authorize the core team to rebalance ETH positions or withdraw ETH into the development fund if needed.

#### Sender (Treasury): 0xe1F03b7B0eBf84e9B9f62a1dB40f1Efb8FaA7d22
#### Receiver (Dev Fund): 0xDfF2aeA378e41632E45306A6dE26A7E0Fd93AB07

The funds can be moved back to SiloDAO's treasury at any time via a new governance proposal.

Once funds become available in the development fund, we can vote to determine:
1. Silos that will receive ETH
2. ETH weight per voted silo

Why deploy ETH to silos?
- Silos need initial liquidity to generate traction.
- ETH sits idle in the treasury.
- It garners trust in the protocol and encourages prospective depositors to deposit and borrow.
- It opens up co-marketing opportunities with other projects.

# SIP-4: Reimbursing the development fund for buying CVX **INTRODUCTION ** Today we announced that the core team had used the development fund to buy CVX. The move comes in a plan to build market liquidity for Silo on Curve V2. So far the core team has purchased around \~241,000 CVX for roughly 4.25M USDC.
The amount is held in the development fund now: [https://etherscan.io/address/0xDfF2aeA378e41632E45306A6dE26A7E0Fd93AB07]()
Announcement: Silo buying CVX: [https://medium.com/silo-protocol/silo-enters-the-curve-wars-91ba701b8170]()
Over the coming weeks, the core team aims to buy more CVX until the DAO owns roughly 300,000 – 350,000 CVX through the development fund. We expect the cost to reach \~5.5M USDC in total.
The CVX holdings will be used to direct CRV emissions to a SILO-FRAX pool on Curve V2. Revenues generated from locking CVX (bribes, farming, etc.) will be directed to the DAO.
Read a governance proposal explaining why purchase CVX: [https://gov.silo.finance/t/use-treasury-funds-to-buy-convex-cvx/213]()

**VOTE** You are voting to transfer 2,553 WETH (equivalent of $6M) in WETH to the development fund. We used the lowest ETH price in the last 30 days to calculate the amount in ETH.
Funds will reimburse the development fund for buying 4.25M USDC worth of CVX. The remainder will be kept in the development fund to keep buying CVX until we reach our goal of 300,000-350,000 CVX. Once we’ve reached our goal, excess funds will be returned to the treasury in WETH.

**TRANSACTION** Transfer 2,553 WETH From: 0xe1F03b7B0eBf84e9B9f62a1dB40f1Efb8FaA7d22 To: 0xDfF2aeA378e41632E45306A6dE26A7E0Fd93AB07

# SIIP-3: Transferring funds to Silo’s development fund A few days ago we shared with you a proposal to create Silo’s stablecoin. The project is part of a larger vision to build a Defi protocol that consists of lending and credit markets. To achieve this vision and remain competitive with large protocols in space, Silo needs to secure enough funds to extend its runway to around 2 years.
Proposal details:
- Expand the team: We plan to add 3 smart contract engineers, 1 business development lead, 2 front-end engineers, and 1 product designer to the team.
- Vest Future Contributors’ token allocation to the development fund’s multisig wallet instead of the DAO’s treasury. This action doesn’t change the current token allocation for Future Contributors but rather makes it easier for the team to manage new hires’ vesting contracts. Instead of asking the DAO to approve each hire’s token allocation, change an existing contributor’s allocation or revoke it if a hire leaves the team for any reason, the core team can take such actions in a timely manner. This is crucial to expanding and maintaining a strong team.
This proposal consists of two transactions:
**Transaction 1** Transfer 3,125 ETH (equivalent of $7.5M) from DAO to the development fund (0xDfF2aeA378e41632E45306A6dE26A7E0Fd93AB07).
**Transaction 2 ** Replace the address vesting Future Contributors’ allocation from the DAO’s treasury to the development fund’s multisig (0xDfF2aeA378e41632E45306A6dE26A7E0Fd93AB07).

- Vest Future Contributors’ token allocation to the development fund’s multisig wallet instead of the DAO’s treasury. This action doesn’t change the current token allocation for Future Contributors but rather makes it easier for the team to manage new hires’ vesting contracts. Instead of asking the DAO to approve each hire’s token allocation, change an existing contributor’s allocation or revoke it if a hire leaves the team for any reason, the core team can take such actions in a timely manner. This is crucial to expanding and maintaining a strong team.
This proposal consists of two transactions:
**Transaction 1** Transfer 3,125 ETH (equivalent of $7.5M) from DAO to the development fund (0xDfF2aeA378e41632E45306A6dE26A7E0Fd93AB07).
**Transaction 2 ** Replace the address vesting Future Contributors’ allocation from the DAO’s treasury to the development fund’s multisig (0xDfF2aeA378e41632E45306A6dE26A7E0Fd93AB07).

SIP-5: Configure a Silo for USDC on Ethereum Mainnet

This proposal consists of multiple actions. If the vote is passed, actions will be executed on-chain, resulting in the configuration of a borrow/lend market for the following token asset:
USDC
0xA0b86991c6218b36c1d19D4a2e9Eb0cE3606eB48

## Action 1: Set up a price feed for the proposed Silo in price provider
0x8ad599c3a0ff1de082011efddc58f1908eb6e6d8
The action registers a price feed (pool) for the token asset.

## Action 2: Choose a price provider
UniswapV3PriceProvider

The action registers a price provider in the protocol's repository of price providers. The Repository manages price providers and maps assets to their price providers.

## Action 3: Set up maxLTV/LT for an asset (USDC)
Max Loan to Value (LTV) = 90%
Liquidation Threshold (LT) = 95%

## Action 4: Set up maxLTV/LT for a bridge asset (ETH) in a Silo
Max Loan to Value (LTV) = 80%
Liquidation Threshold (LT) = 85%

## Action 5: Set up interest rate model for an asset (USDC)

# SIP-3 (New): Transferring funds to Silo’s development fund
PS: This proposal replaces SIIP-03. SIIP-03 will not reach quorum because of limited delegated voting pool at the time of SIIP-03 creation.



We’ve recently shared with you a proposal to create Silo’s stablecoin. The project is part of a larger vision to build a Defi protocol that consists of lending and credit markets. To achieve this vision and remain competitive with large protocols in space, Silo needs to secure enough funds to extend its runway to around 2 years.



Proposal details:

1. Expand the team: We plan to add 3 smart contract engineers, 1 business development lead, 2 front-end engineers, and 1 product designer to the team.

2. Vest Future Contributors’ token allocation to the development fund’s multisig wallet instead of the DAO’s treasury. This action doesn’t change the current token allocation for Future Contributors but rather makes it easier for the team to manage new hires’ vesting contracts. Instead of asking the DAO to approve each hire’s token allocation, change an existing contributor’s allocation or revoke it if a hire leaves the team for any reason, the core team can take such actions in a timely manner. This is crucial to expanding and maintaining a strong team.


<!-- -->



This proposal consists of two transactions:



**Transaction 1**

Transfer 2,925 ETH (equivalent of $7.5M) from the DAO to the development fund.



From: 0xe1F03b7B0eBf84e9B9f62a1dB40f1Efb8FaA7d22

To: 0xDfF2aeA378e41632E45306A6dE26A7E0Fd93AB07



We used a 14-day price average to calculate the amount in ETH.



**Transaction 2 **

Replace the address that vests the Future Contributors’ SILO allocation with that of the development fund’s multisig 0xDfF2aeA378e41632E45306A6dE26A7E0Fd93AB07.



How to vote



PS: You can only vote if you delegated your vote before the proposal was created.



1. Visit Silo’s governance page on Tally

    [https://www.withtally.com/governance/eip155:1:0xA89163F7B2D68A8fbA6Ca36BEEd32Bd4f3EeAf61](<https://www.withtally.com/governance/eip155:1:0xA89163F7B2D68A8fbA6Ca36BEEd32Bd4f3EeAf61>)

2. Visit the proposal page.

3. Click on Vote, and select an option.

4. Confirm the transaction
